text: Interview Coder just leaked their users' full names, compensation details, location, and where they got offers from. And they didn't do this in some random dark web data breach. They literally posted it themselves on the homepage of their website. Let me show you what I mean. Okay, so Interview Coder is kind of this famous, you know, AI cheating platform where you can cheat in technical interviews. It's supposed to be able to answer the questions for you. It's ran by this guy right here, Roy Lee, who's just now hidden by the UI. And anyways, they've bragged about making, you know, millions of dollars. And the other day they made this tweet saying, Hey, today we shipped, you know, an offer gallery real offers, real users. Yeah, definitely real users. As you're going to see, let's go to Amazon. Especially if you're using an AI cheating tool, you probably don't want your name released on the internet when you literally cheated unethically in the interview.
